abacus.help.step1: Click beads to move them towards the center bar to count.
abacus.help.step4: Each column represents a different value, starting from 1s on the left up to 1,000,000,000s!
abacus.help.title: How to Use the Abacus
about.privacy.desc: ClassRex is built with a "Privacy First" philosophy. Unlike many modern web apps, we don't use cookies, trackers, or cloud databases. Everything you create—from student lists to customized tools—stays exclusively on your device's Local Storage.
about.privacy.title: 100% Privacy by Design
about.speed.desc: Because no data is sent to a server, ClassRex is lightning fast. It works 100% offline once loaded, ensuring your classroom flow is never interrupted by poor connectivity.
about.speed.dino: ClassRex is an homage to the resilience of the Chrome Dino—providing a reliable experience even when there is no internet access. Just like the Dino keeps going, so does your classroom!
about.speed.title: Fast, Reliable & Offline
about.support.desc: ClassRex is free to use and ad-free. If you find these tools helpful and would like to support their development, please consider becoming a patron.
about.support.title: Support the Project
about.teachers.desc: ClassRex is a collection of high-fidelity interactive tools designed to make classroom management and teaching more engaging. From Random Name Pickers to Science Simulations, every tool is crafted for the modern classroom.
about.teachers.title: Built for Teachers
about.title: About ClassRex
behaviourdiamond.context_text: The Behaviour Diamond helps kids make great choices! Students show their feelings and choices by moving up and down the diamond levels during the school day.

privacy.contact.desc: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or how ClassRex handles data, please reach out to us via our Patreon page.
privacy.contact.title: Contact
privacy.section1.desc1: ClassRex is designed with a "Privacy First" and "Local First" architecture. All data you enter into the application (such as student names, custom word lists, class schedules, and settings) is stored locally on your device using your browser's Local Storage.
privacy.section1.desc2: We do not have access to this data, nor is it ever transmitted to our servers or any third-party databases. You have complete ownership and control over your classroom data.
privacy.section1.title: 1. Data Storage & Local Processing
privacy.section2.desc1: ClassRex does not use cloud databases for storing user-generated content. We do not use invasive tracking pixels. The application is served statically, meaning it can function entirely offline once loaded.
privacy.section2.desc2: As with most website hosting services, standard server logs are maintained by our hosting provider. These logs may include IP addresses, browser types, and access times for security and maintenance purposes, but they are not linked to your classroom data.
privacy.section2.title: 2. Cloud Services & Analytics
privacy.section3.desc: No cookies are used at all on ClassRex. We do not use advertising cookies, third-party trackers, or cross-site tracking scripts. Browser local storage is used strictly to save your settings and classroom data on your own device.
privacy.section3.title: 3. Cookies and Tracking
privacy.section4.apps: Australian Privacy Principles (APPs): We satisfy the 13 APPs under the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth), particularly regarding anonymity (APP 2) and data security (APP 11).
privacy.section4.coppa: COPPA: We do not collect personal information from children under 13.
privacy.section4.desc: ClassRex is an educational tool designed for teachers to use in the classroom. Because all data is stored locally on the teacher's device and no personal information is transmitted to our servers, ClassRex inherently complies with:
privacy.section4.ferpa: FERPA: Student records remain within the school's control on the teacher's device.
privacy.section4.gdpr: GDPR: We act as a "Zero-Knowledge" provider; you remain the sole controller of your data.
privacy.section4.title: 4. Regulatory Compliance (COPPA, GDPR, FERPA, APPs)
privacy.section5.access: Right to Access & Portability: You can download a complete copy of all your data at any time via the "Export Backup" feature.
privacy.section5.desc: Under global privacy regulations, you have the following rights, which ClassRex facilitates through its "Configuration" tool:
privacy.section5.erasure: Right to Erasure (The "Right to be Forgotten"): You can permanently delete all data stored by ClassRex using the "Clear Database" button in the settings.
privacy.section5.rectification: Right to Rectification: You can directly edit or update any information (such as class lists or words) within the application.
privacy.section5.title: 5. Your Rights (Access, Portability, Erasure)
privacy.section6.desc: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Any changes will be reflected on this page. Because we do not collect your email address or contact information, we cannot notify you directly of changes; please review this page periodically.
privacy.section6.title: 6. Changes to this Policy
privacy.title: Privacy Policy

reactionTime.help.step2: The screen will turn Red—this means WAIT. Don't click yet!
reactionTime.help.step3: When the screen turns Green, click as fast as you possibly can!
reactionTime.help.step4: Check your Stats in the sidebar to see your average and best reaction times.

starchart.board.empty.desc: Please load a class with student names from the Class selector at the top to start tracking rewards!
starchart.board.empty.title: Ready to award stars?
starchart.help.step1: Award Stars: Click the small plus (+ ⭐) or minus (- ⭐) buttons on any student card to award stars.
starchart.help.step2: Select a Student: Click on a student's name card to select them for shopping. Their card will highlight.
starchart.help.step3: Preview Items: Tap any affordable item in the shop at the bottom. It temporarily applies to the student's card.
starchart.help.step4: Confirm Buy: Tap the same item a second time to purchase it! Stars will be deducted immediately. There are no refunds.
starchart.help.title: How to use Star Chart:
starchart.info.about: By default, every student begins with 0 stars, a plain white card background, standard dark outline borders, and zero emoji badges. All items in the store can be unlocked with stars.
starchart.info.context: The Star Chart is a powerful visual rewards engine designed to gamify good behavior and goal achievement. By earning stars, children practice simple financial literacy and spending decisions in a controlled, supportive environment. The customization element gives students deep ownership and pride over their digital identity inside the classroom.
starchart.info.title: Tool Information
starchart.shop.subtitle: Select a student above to buy upgrades!
starchart.shop.title: Star Store
stopwatch.help.step1: Click the Main Button to start or pause the stopwatch.
stopwatch.help.step2: Use the Flag Icon to record a lap point.
stopwatch.help.step3: The History on the right shows your lap times.
stopwatch.help.title: Stopwatch
stopwatch.history.title: Lap History
storystarters.help.step2: Click the button to get a story idea.
storystarters.help.step3: Look at your old ideas in the list.
storystarters.help.title: How to Play
storystarters.history.title: Old Ideas
storystarters.prompt.animal_coin: The ancient coin allowed whoever held it to speak to animals.
storystarters.prompt.blue_drops: The sun was shining when suddenly it started raining blue drops...
storystarters.prompt.brave_kitten: A brave kitten wanted to climb the highest tree...
storystarters.prompt.bug_spaceship: The spaceship landed in our backyard, but it was the size of a bug.
storystarters.prompt.candy_world: The secret door in the library led to a world of candy.
storystarters.prompt.clock_thirteen: Every time the clock struck thirteen, time would freeze.
storystarters.prompt.deleting_invention: The invention was meant to clean rooms, but it started deleting them instead.
storystarters.prompt.floating_city: The city floated above the clouds, anchored by massive chains that were rusting.
storystarters.prompt.forgotten_event: Nobody remembered the event, except for the photographs that proved it happened.
storystarters.prompt.forgotten_memories: The artifact hummed with a frequency that unlocked forgotten memories.
storystarters.prompt.friendly_bear: In a dark forest, a friendly bear woke up and...
storystarters.prompt.grandma_superhero: I discovered my grandma was a retired superhero.
storystarters.prompt.invisible_ink: A message in a bottle washed ashore, written in invisible ink.
storystarters.prompt.magic_bone: Once upon a time, a little dog found a magic bone...
storystarters.prompt.mirror_worlds: Every reflection in the mirror showed a slightly different world.
storystarters.prompt.missing_moon: When I looked out my window, the moon was missing!
storystarters.prompt.mysterious_signal: A mysterious signal interrupted every screen in the city.
storystarters.prompt.paintbrush: A magical paintbrush drew things that came to life.
storystarters.prompt.self_writing_book: The last library on Earth contained a book that wrote itself.
storystarters.prompt.shiny_coin: Under the sea, a tiny fish found a shiny coin...
storystarters.prompt.shrinking_perimeter: We were warned never to go past the perimeter, but the perimeter was shrinking.
storystarters.prompt.sky_x: The map had an X, but it wasn't on the ground, it was in the sky.
storystarters.prompt.storm_creatures: The storm brought more than rain; it brought creatures of wind and lightning.
storystarters.prompt.swapped_voices: The zoo animals swapped voices for a day.
storystarters.prompt.tail_backpack: The new kid at school had a tail sticking out of his backpack.
storystarters.prompt.time_left: The chronometer didn't tell time; it told you how much time you had left.
storystarters.prompt.toy_box: The old toy box in the attic started making a strange noise...
storystarters.prompt.train_ticket: We found a train ticket with tomorrow's date and an unknown destination.
storystarters.prompt.walking_opposite: My shadow started walking the opposite way I was going!
storystarters.prompt.winking_painting: The painting in the museum winked at me when no one was looking.
storystarters.title: Story Ideas
teachermath.help.step1: Enter points like (2, 3) or formulas like y=x in the sidebar.
teachermath.help.step2: Click anywhere on the grid to add a point at that location.
teachermath.help.step3: Use the slider at the bottom to change the Grid Size.
teachermath.help.step4: The grid automatically shuffles colors for each new element.
teachermath.title: Formulas
tool.abacus.info: Shift beads across rows to represent numbers from ones to billions. A classic tool for understanding place value and addition.
tool.abacus.name: Abacus
tool.balanceequations.info: Practice balancing equations by placing weights on both sides of the scale. Learn the concept of equality in algebra. Balance: Both sides of the equation must equal the same value for the scale to balance.
tool.balanceequations.name: Balance Equations
tool.behaviourdiamond.description: Visual choices and behavior tracking chart
tool.behaviourdiamond.name: Behaviour Diamond
tool.binarynumbers.info: A fun way to learn how computers use 0s and 1s to make numbers. Place Value: Each card has a value. Add them up to find the total number.
tool.binarynumbers.name: Binary Numbers
tool.binarytool.name: Binary Tool
tool.chemicalfireworks.info: Explore how different chemical elements produce different colors when heated. Learn about atomic emission spectra. Experiment: Drag different elements into the flame to see the resulting firework color.
tool.chemicalfireworks.name: Chemical Fireworks
tool.classrex.info: Control ClassRex as they run through the prehistoric world. Jump over obstacles and see how far you can go! Difficulty: The game speed increases over time, testing your reaction speed.
tool.classrex.name: ClassRex
tool.clock.info: A versatile clock featuring both traditional analogue and modern digital displays. Toggle between 'Real Time' to show the current system clock or 'Edit Mode' to manually set the time. Interactive Hands: Switch to Manual Mode and drag the clock hands to explore time relationships. Natural Language: Observe how the text display synthesizes the time into conversational English as you move the hands.
tool.clock.name: Analogue Digital Clock
tool.colourhunt.info: Find objects in the classroom or images that match the target color. A great way to learn color names and shades. Timer: Race against the clock to find as many matching colors as possible!
tool.colourhunt.name: Colour Hunt
tool.colourpicker.info: A simple tool for selecting and comparing colors. Explore HEX, RGB, and HSL values for any color you choose. Palettes: Generate complementary or analogous color schemes automatically.
tool.colourpicker.name: Colour Picker
tool.config.info: Manage your application preferences, class lists, and data backups here.
tool.config.name: Config
tool.countdown.info: Set a timer and choose from multiple visualization modes: a classic circle, a progress bar, a sand timer, or a flip clock. Features audible alarms and a 'Wait-Time' spinner for classroom management.
tool.countdown.name: Countdown
tool.crossword.info: Solve crossword puzzles based on different themes or your own custom word lists. Use the clues to fill in the grid. Hints: Get help with a single letter or an entire word if you're stuck.
tool.crossword.name: Crossword
tool.dailyschedule.info: Visual Progress The blue background on each activity shows real-time progress as the lesson proceeds (only for the current day). Persistence Your schedules are saved locally in this browser. You can maintain a different schedule for each day of the week.
tool.dailyschedule.name: Daily Schedule
tool.diceroller.info: Click dice buttons to add them to your pool. You can add up to 24 dice! Roll the pool to see random values and their total sum.
tool.diceroller.name: Dice Roller
tool.ecosystem.info: Model the complex relationships in an ecosystem. Track the populations of producers, consumers, and decomposers over time. Balance: See how changes in one population affect the entire food web.
tool.ecosystem.name: Ecosystem
tool.ecosystemsimulation.name: Ecosystem Simulation
tool.emojimatch.info: Test your memory with this emoji-based matching game. Flip cards to find matching pairs and clear the board. Grid Size: Choose different grid sizes to increase the difficulty of the memory challenge.
tool.emojimatch.name: Emoji Match
tool.emotionpicker.info: A simple way for students to express their current emotional state. Select an emoji to see a larger check-in message, promoting emotional awareness and empathy.
tool.emotionpicker.name: Emotion Picker
tool.eventcalendar.info: Toggle between the Calendar view for planning and the Countdowns view for tracking upcoming milestones in real-time.
tool.eventcalendar.name: Event Calendar
tool.examclock.info: Designed for exams and tests. Includes dedicated 'Reading Time' which pauses the clock automatically, and a configurable 'Warning Period' that pulses the display when time is running low to alert students.
tool.examclock.name: Exam Clock
tool.findtheword.info: Find the target words hidden in the grid. Click and drag to select a word. Words can be horizontal, vertical, or diagonal! Custom Lists: Import your own spelling lists to generate unique puzzles.
tool.findtheword.name: Find The Word
tool.flipcoin.info: Click the coin or flip button to start the toss. The coin uses real physics-based rotation! Track results with the live chart and download data as CSV.
tool.flipcoin.name: Flip Coin
tool.fractiontool.info: Explore fractions using both area models (Circle) and linear models (Number Line) simultaneously. Click directly on the circle segments or the number line to quickly jump to a value.
tool.fractiontool.name: Fraction Tool
tool.fractionwall.info: Compare different fraction layers to find equivalent values. Highlight specific segments to see how they align across the wall.
tool.fractionwall.name: Fraction Wall
tool.groupmaker.info: Select a class and choose how you want to group your students. The tool will handle the shuffle for you! Choose between a fixed number of groups or a specific number of students per group.
tool.groupmaker.name: Random Group Maker
tool.groupnamegenerator.description: Creative and Inspiring Identity for Student Teams
tool.groupnamegenerator.info: Generate five fun, school-appropriate group names at the click of a button. Perfect for project teams, house groups, or classroom competitive play.
tool.groupnamegenerator.name: Group Name Generator
tool.groupscoreboard.info: Keep track of team points with ease. Add, rename, or remove groups to match your lesson needs. Scores are saved automatically and can be reset for each new activity.
tool.groupscoreboard.name: Group Score Board
tool.guessinggame.description: Multiple choice emoji and pattern quiz
tool.guessinggame.info: Select a topic, then you'll be shown an emoji. Guess the correct word that matches the emoji! Timer: You have 10 seconds to answer each question. The faster you answer, the better!
tool.guessinggame.name: Guessing Game
tool.higherorlower.info: Guess whether the next card or number will be higher or lower than the current one. A simple way to explore probability and number relationships. Streak: See how many correct guesses you can make in a row!
tool.higherorlower.name: Higher Or Lower
tool.hundredschart.description: Interactive base-10 exploration and pattern finding
tool.hundredschart.info: Use 'Hide Mode' to conceal numbers for guessing games. Use colors to highlight multiples or skip-counting patterns. Quickly highlight multiples of 2, 3, 5, or 10 with the pattern cascade buttons.
tool.hundredschart.name: Hundreds Chart
tool.imagereveal.info: A fun game where an image is slowly revealed. Guess what the image is before it's fully uncovered! Modes: Reveal tiles one by one or use the automatic progressive reveal.
tool.imagereveal.name: Image Reveal
tool.inkdiffusion.info: Observe the process of diffusion as ink molecules move through water. Explore how temperature and concentration affect the rate of diffusion. Brownian Motion: Watch how individual particles move randomly to eventually create a uniform distribution.
tool.inkdiffusion.name: Ink Diffusion
tool.lettertracing.info: Practice writing letters with this interactive tracing tool. Follow the guides to learn correct stroke order. Feedback: Real-time feedback helps students stay within the lines.
tool.lettertracing.name: Letter Tracing
tool.mabblocks.info: Drag blocks from the bank onto the workspace to explore place value. Drag a block off the workspace to remove it. Use the sort button to automatically organize your blocks into hundreds, tens, and ones columns.
tool.mabblocks.name: MAB Blocks
tool.marblecounting.info: A fun way for early learners to practice counting. Count the marbles in the jar and select the correct number. Subitizing: Helps develop the ability to see a small amount of objects and know how many there are without counting.
tool.marblecounting.name: Marble Counting
tool.marblejar.info: Click inside the jar to add a marble. Click on an existing marble to remove it. Reach the marble goal to trigger a celebratory reward for the class!
tool.marblejar.name: Marble Jar Reward
tool.metronome.info: Keep a steady beat with this precision metronome. Adjust the tempo (BPM) and time signature to suit your music. Presets: Quickly switch between common tempos like Allegro, Moderato, or Adagio.
tool.metronome.name: Metronome
tool.missingaddition.info: Find the missing number to complete the addition equation. A great way to practice number bonds and inverse operations. Difficulty: Choose between sums up to 10 or 100.
tool.missingaddition.name: Missing Addition
tool.missingdivision.info: Find the missing number to complete the division equation. Difficulty: Choose between quotients up to 10 or 100.
tool.missingdivision.name: Missing Division
tool.missingmultiplication.info: Find the missing number to complete the multiplication equation. Difficulty: Choose between products up to 10 or 100.
tool.missingmultiplication.name: Missing Multiplication
tool.missingsubtraction.info: Find the missing number to complete the subtraction equation. Difficulty: Choose between numbers up to 10 or 100.
tool.missingsubtraction.name: Missing Subtraction
tool.moneytool.info: Explore money and learn how to make change. Drag coins and notes to reach the target amount. Currencies: Support for multiple currencies including GBP, USD, and AUD.
tool.moneytool.name: Money Tool
tool.namepicker.description: Random Student Selector with Wheel & List animations
tool.namepicker.info: A fun way to pick students for activities. Switch between a spinning wheel and a scrolling list. Load your class lists to pick from your students automatically.
tool.namepicker.name: Name Picker
tool.numberline.description: Visualize sequences, addition, and subtraction jumps
tool.numberline.info: Click a number to add or remove a pin. Click and drag between two numbers to create a jump. Adjust the range and step size to explore different number scales.
tool.numberline.name: Number Line
tool.numberspinner.description: Interactive Probability and Random Selection Wheel
tool.numberspinner.info: Set your min and max values. The wheel will automatically divide into segments. Track every spin in history and download CSV report.
tool.numberspinner.name: Number Spinner
tool.poolgame.info: Explore momentum, collisions, and angles in this physics-based pool simulation. Aim and shoot to sink the balls! Trajectories: Use the real-time prediction lines to plan your shots based on physics principles.
tool.poolgame.name: Pool Game
tool.qrcodegenerator.info: Convert any URL or text into a QR code that students can scan. A quick way to share resources or websites. Customization: Change the color and size of the QR code before downloading or presenting it.
tool.qrcodegenerator.name: QR Code Generator
tool.reactiontime.info: Wait for the screen to turn green, then click as fast as you can! Track your reaction time across multiple attempts. Analytics: View your average, best, and worst times in the history panel.
tool.reactiontime.name: Reaction Time
tool.revealword.info: A fun way to introduce new vocabulary. The word is hidden, and letters are revealed one by one. Can the students guess the word before it's fully shown? Custom Words: Add your own words or phrases to tailor the game to your current lesson.
tool.revealword.name: Reveal Word
tool.sandsim.info: A particle-based sandbox where different elements interact. Experiment with gravity, heat, and chemistry to see what happens when you mix different materials. Emitters: Use the generator mode to create a continuous stream of elements.
tool.sandsim.name: Sand Simulation
tool.sandsimulation.name: Sand Simulation
tool.seatingplan.name: Seating Plan Generator
tool.simongame.info: Watch the sequence of colors and sounds, then repeat it back in the same order. How long a sequence can you remember? Difficulty: The sequence gets longer with each successful round.
tool.simongame.name: Simon Game
tool.simonsays.description: A high-energy movement and attention game
tool.simonsays.info: Follow Simon's instructions ONLY if they say "Simon says...". If they don't, stay perfectly still! Auto Mode: Enable Auto Mode to let the tool generate commands automatically at your chosen speed.
tool.simonsays.name: Simon Says (Classroom)
tool.songmaker.info: Create your own music with this interactive step sequencer. Click on the grid to add notes. Choose from different instruments and adjust the tempo. Settings: Change the instrument, scale, and grid size in the settings panel.
tool.songmaker.name: Song Maker
tool.soundlevel.info: Start monitoring to see the real-time volume. The green ring shows current noise, and the grey ring shows a 2-second rolling average. Drag the purple marker on the gauge to set your 'Danger' threshold.
tool.soundlevel.name: Sound Level
tool.spelling.info: A comprehensive spelling tool. Create custom lists or use the default ones. Practice spelling words with audio feedback. Modes: Practice mode for learning, and Test mode for evaluation.
tool.spelling.name: Spelling
tool.springscales.info: Learn how to measure force using a spring scale. Explore the relationship between mass, gravity, and spring extension. Hooke's Law: Observe how the extension of the spring is proportional to the force applied.
tool.springscales.name: Spring Scales
tool.standingwave.info: Explore the physics of standing waves. Adjust frequency and amplitude to see how they affect wave patterns. Nodes: Identify nodes and antinodes in the wave visualization.
tool.standingwave.name: Standing Wave
tool.standingwavesynthesis.name: Standing Wave Synthesis
tool.starchart.description: Star reward store and student card customization board
tool.starchart.name: Star Chart
tool.stopwatch.info: Track time with millisecond precision. Use the Lap feature to record intermediate times without stopping the main clock. You can export your lap history as a CSV file for data analysis in science experiments or PE activities.
tool.stopwatch.name: Stopwatch
tool.storystarters.info: Overcome writer's block with aged-tailored creative prompts. Select a year level to get sentences that match the literacy expectations for that age group.
tool.storystarters.name: Story Starters
tool.sudoku.info: A classic logic puzzle. Fill the grid so that every row, column, and subgrid contains all digits from 1 to 9 (or 1 to 4/6 for easier levels). Hints: Use the hint button if you get stuck, but try to solve it on your own first!
tool.sudoku.name: Sudoku
tool.teachermath.info: Click the grid to drop points or type equations like 'y = 2x + 1' in the sidebar. Explore coordinates and equations on an interactive Cartesian plane.
tool.teachermath.name: Cartesian Plane
tool.thermalconduction.info: Observe how heat moves through different materials. Compare the conductivity of metals, wood, and other substances. Simulation: Apply heat to one end of a rod and track the temperature change along its length.
tool.thermalconduction.name: Thermal Conduction
tool.timestable.info: Master your multiplication tables through interactive practice. Choose a table to focus on or challenge yourself with the mixed mode. Modes: Sequence mode for learning, and Shuffle mode for testing recall.
tool.timestable.name: Times Table
tool.tournaments.info: Run single or double elimination tournaments for your class. Use the Elo system to track skill levels over time. Perfect for classroom games, spelling bees, or sports.
tool.tournaments.name: Tournaments
tool.typinggame.info: Improve your typing speed and accuracy. Type the words as they appear on the screen before they reach the bottom! Stats: Track your words per minute (WPM) and accuracy percentage.
tool.typinggame.name: Typing Game
tool.wordbuilder.info: Build words by dragging letters or phonemes into the workspace. Perfect for early literacy and phonics practice. Audio: Hear the sounds of the letters as you build your words.
tool.wordbuilder.name: Word Builder
tool.wouldyourather.description: A high-engagement debate and ice-breaker tool
tool.wouldyourather.info: A classic ice-breaker or debate game. Present two choices to the class and have students vote by moving to different sides of the room or raising hands. Tailored for Ages: Questions are carefully selected to be age-appropriate for different year levels.
tool.wouldyourather.name: Would You Rather
typinggame.help.step1: Pick a Word List in the settings.
typinggame.help.step2: Type the letters on the space ships to blast them!
typinggame.help.step3: If you make a mistake, use Backspace.
typinggame.help.title: How to Play
typinggame.subtitle: Type to win
typinggame.title: Typing Game
wordbuilder.help.step1: Click a Sound to add it to your word.
wordbuilder.help.step2: Vowels are red and consonants are blue.
wordbuilder.help.step4: Click a sound on the board to remove it.
wordbuilder.help.title: Build Words
wordbuilder.subtitle: Make words with sounds
wordbuilder.title: Word Builder
wordpanel.title: Word Manager
wyr.help.step1: Choose your year level to get started.
wyr.help.step3: Discuss your choices with the class!
wyr.history.title: History
wyr.title: Would You Rather
